### Your current environment
The output of python collect_env.py ```text Your output of `python collect_env.py` here ```
### Your code version
The commit id or version of vllm ```text 0.24.0 ```
The commit id or version of vllm-omni ```text v0.24.0rc1 ```
### 🐛 Describe the bug pytest -s -v tests/examples/online_serving/test_qwen3_omni.py::test_send_multimodal_request_002[omni_server0] add local address for video ``` @hardware_test(res={"cuda": "H100", "rocm": "MI325"}, num_cards=2) @pytest.mark.parametrize("omni_server", test_params, indirect=True) def test_send_multimodal_request_002(omni_server) -> None: command = common_args + [ "--query-type", "use_video", "--model", omni_server.model, "--video-path", "/rebase/vllm-omni/sample_demo_1.mp4", "--prompt", "What are the main activities shown in this video?", ] result = run_cmd(command) text_content_tmp = extract_content_after_keyword("Chat completion output from text:", result) text_content = strip_trailing_audio_saved_line(text_content_tmp) # Verify text output same as audio output wav_path = extract_last_audio_saved_path(result) audio_content = convert_audio_file_to_text(output_path=f"./{wav_path}") print(f"text content is: {text_content}") print(f"audio content is: {audio_content}") assert all(keyword in text_content for keyword in ["baby", "book"]), ( "The output does not contain any of the keywords." ) similarity = cosine_similarity_text(audio_content.lower(), text_content.lower()) print(f"similarity is: {similarity}") assert similarity > 0.8, "The audio content is not same as the text" # TODO: Verify the E2E latency after confirmation baseline. ``` log ``` (APIServer pid=2675047) INFO: 127.0.0.1:34756 - "POST /v1/chat/completions HTTP/1.1" 200 OK All output: INFO 07-02 12:22:06 [patch.py:252] NVFP4 W4A4 weight_scale NaN-clamp: installed. Chat completion output from text: Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Audio saved to audio_chatcmpl-a97686a01f3c3c65_0.wav INFO 07-02 12:22:38 [patch.py:252] NVFP4 W4A4 weight_scale NaN-clamp: installed. text content is: Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe Joe

comments.author: IneshReddy249
comments.body: Hi! I have access to an A100 and would love to dig into this hallucination issue. Is anyone currently working on a fix, or can I pick this up?

comments.author: akshatvishu
comments.body:
Hey @zhumingjue138  ; You're running this with  the default `--run-level=core_model`, which now patches `stage_config_path` configs to use  `load_format: dummy`. That behavior was extended to online serving in #4354. Since this test asserts real semantic output (`baby`, `book`), it must be run with real weights:

```bash
pytest -s -v tests/examples/online_serving/test_qwen3_omni.py::test_send_multimodal_request_002[omni_server0] --run-level=full_model 
```

if you debug :
```bash
export VLLM_LOGGING_LEVEL=DEBUG
pytest -s -v \
    tests/examples/online_serving/test_qwen3_omni.py::test_send_multimodal_request_002[omni_server0] \
    2>&1 | tee qwen3_test.log
``` 

You can see that all 3 stages are loading with dummy weights (I've modified the config file to run all 3 stages on same device , since I am running this on a single mi300x) : 
```bash
(APIServer pid=5625) DEBUG 07-02 20:47:14 [utils.py:585] stage_configs: [
  {
    'stage_id': 0, 
    'stage_type': 'llm', 
    'engine_args': {
      'model_arch': 'Qwen3OmniMoeForConditionalGeneration', 
      'engine_output_type': 'latent', 
      'custom_process_next_stage_input_func': 'vllm_omni.model_executor.stage_input_processors.qwen3_omni.thinker2talker_full_payload', 
      'active_stream_window': 0, 
      'gpu_memory_utilization': 0.55, 
      'max_num_seqs': 1, 
      'max_num_batched_tokens': 32768, 
      'max_model_len': 32768, 
      'mm_processor_cache_gb': 0, 
      'load_format': 'dummy',                        # DUMMY WEIGHT
      'async_chunk': False, 
      'async_scheduling': True, 
      'model_stage': 'thinker', 
      'worker_type': 'ar', 
      'scheduler_cls': 'vllm_omni.core.sched.omni_ar_scheduler.OmniARAsyncScheduler', 
      'hf_config_name': 'thinker_config', 
      'subparser': 'serve', 
      'model_tag': 'Qwen/Qwen3-Omni-30B-A3B-Instruct', 
      'host': '127.0.0.1', 
      'port': 8091, 
      'omni': True
    }, 
    'runtime': {'process': True, 'devices': '0', 'num_replicas': 1, 'require

comments.author: yenuo26
comments.body: I verified this in my local A100 environment and was able to pass the test case. Based on the returned output, the issue appears to be more due to the user not setting the correct run-level. This issue has been closed as "invalid"

title: [Bug]: A100, tests/examples/online_serving/test_qwen3_omni.py::test_send_multimodal_request_002[omni_server0] - AssertionError: The output does not contain any of the keywords.
